summary: The BEC conjecture remains open in general. Since the problem was posted, Cames van Batenburg and Kang proved it for $K_{2,t}$-free graphs satisfying $\Delta(G_1) > 17t \cdot \Delta(G_2)$, and also for pairs of graphs with even girth at least 10 when at least one graph has $\Delta \geq 940060$.

since_posted.title: Packing Graphs of Bounded Codegree
since_posted.authors: Wouter Cames van Batenburg, Ross J. Kang
since_posted.claim: Proves the BEC conjecture under the additional hypothesis that $G_1$ contains no $K_{2,t}$ and $\Delta(G_1) > 17t \cdot \Delta(G_2)$.

since_posted.title: The Bollobás-Eldridge-Catlin conjecture for even girth at least 10
since_posted.authors: Wouter Cames van Batenburg, Ross J. Kang
since_posted.claim: Proves the BEC conjecture when neither $G_1$ nor $G_2$ contains a 4-, 6-, or 8-cycle (even girth $\geq 10$) and at least one has maximum degree $\geq 940060$.

search_queries: Bollobás-Eldridge-Catlin conjecture graph packing BEC conjecture progress proof
BEC conjecture graph packing maximum degree conjecture solved arXiv 2013 2026
graph packing conjecture "(Delta(G1)+1)(Delta(G2)+1) < n+1" recent results
Kostochka Yu graph packing BEC conjecture partial results 2014 2025
notes: The Kaul-Kostochka-Yu (2008) Combinatorica paper on the BEC conjecture predates the 2013 OPG posting and is not included. A Radboud University repository page titled 'Packing two graphs of even girth 10' by the same authors returned HTTP 403 and could not be verified; it may be a published journal version of arXiv:1703.05149. No full proof or disproof of the conjecture was found in the post-2013 literature.
